title: Teyana Taylor Has Best Reaction to Amy Madigan's Oscars 2026 Best Supporting Actress Win
text: Teyana Taylor emerged as a standout presence at the 2026 Academy Awards, delivering memorable moments throughout the ceremony despite not winning her Best Supporting Actress nomination for One Battle After Another. When Amy Madigan won Best Supporting Actress, Taylor was among the first to stand and celebrate. She demonstrated gracious sportsmanship by jumping to her feet to show love for her category mate. Taylor brought K-pop lightstick to the ceremony and enthusiastically waved it during performances. When Michael B. Jordan won Best Actor, Taylor leaped from her seat with visible joy. Her most talked-about moment came when One Battle After Another won Best Picture when she playfully placed director Paul Thomas Anderson in a headlock while celebrating. The article characterizes Taylor as bringing infectious energy to the often-formal ceremony.
